meta.voice: blue-collar contractor talking to peers, no guru language, no em dashes
meta.source: DataForSEO keywords_for_keywords pull 2026-04-18 + Fathom call mining 2026-04-22, 2026-04-23
must_include: one internal link to existing BCT post
one CTA to a BCT tool or newsletter
one hardscape-era story or analogy where natural

tools.gbp-check: nine-dimension GBP score vs competitor median

posts.h1_working: How to get more Google reviews as a contractor (without sounding desperate)
posts.primary_keyword: how to get more google reviews
secondary_keywords: how do i get more google reviews
how to get good google reviews
posts.angle: Most contractors ask once. The ones winning are asking at three moments, with the right wording, and they track who followed through.
posts.notes: Originally planned a Review Request Template Pack. Scrapped 2026-04-19 — was just a duplicate of the post's tap-to-copy scripts. The GHL review-automation walkthrough video (slot 3 lead magnet) is the real value-add.

posts.h1_working: How to pass Google Business Profile video verification (without the back-and-forth)
posts.angle: Exact checklist (signage, truck, keys, business cards, equipment) + common fail reasons. Embed the YouTube walkthrough.
posts.source_note: Multiple clients stalled on this; Kyle 2026-04-22 + Wilson/Darren 2026-04-23

posts.h1_working: How to rank higher on Google Maps as a contractor (7 moves that still work in 2026)
posts.primary_keyword: how to rank higher on google maps
secondary_keywords: how to rank in google maps
how do i rank higher on google maps
how to rank higher in google maps
posts.angle: Proximity is the biggest signal most contractors can't change. Here's what you can change, in order of impact.

posts.h1_working: Map Pack vs Google Ads: where your local clicks actually come from
posts.angle: 60-70% vs 8-10%. Real click-share data. Why you can't outspend your way past a weak GBP.

posts.h1_working: Does your Google Business Profile need a physical address? (service-area vs storefront)
posts.angle: Supreme Scapes case: service-area-only profiles rank weakly outside a tight core. When to add an address, when to hide.

posts.h1_working: How to move your Google Business Profile without losing your rankings
posts.angle: Add location pages → citations → 30-day index window → new GBP → video verify. Packaged as $6K one-time.

posts.h1_working: SEO isn't a lead engine. Here's what it actually is.
posts.angle: Compounding asset vs instant-switch. Set expectations before signing. Sales positioning piece.

posts.h1_working: Duplicate and closed Google listings are killing your rankings (how to clean them up)
posts.angle: Common contractor problem. Step-by-step: identify dupes, request merge, handle closed listings that won't disappear.

posts.h1_working: Start SEO when you're busy, not when you're slow (the contractor timing mistake)
secondary_keywords: when is the best time for seo
posts.angle: Most contractors hire an agency when leads dry up. By then SEO is a 6-month project competing with their panic. Flip it.

posts.h1_working: Best lead generation companies for contractors: honest 2026 breakdown
posts.primary_keyword: best lead generation companies for contractors
posts.angle: Rank and review the real options. What each costs, what you actually get, who should skip each one.

posts.h1_working: The contractor pricing ceiling: why you're probably too cheap
posts.angle: Raise prices when overbooked. Signals you're under-priced. Real ranges by trade.

posts.h1_working: Local Service Ads for contractors: what they cost, how to get badged, when to turn them off
posts.angle: Biggest keyword in this space. Deep-dive pillar post. Walk through setup, badging, pricing mechanics, and the two failure modes that burn contractors fast.

posts.h1_working: Best AI tools for contractors in 2026 (what we actually use at Tekton Growth)
posts.angle: Not a listicle of 100 tools. The 8 we actually run day-to-day — what each replaces, what it costs, what it breaks.

posts.h1_working: How to get leads as a contractor without paying Angi or HomeAdvisor a dime
posts.primary_keyword: how to get leads as a contractor
secondary_keywords: best way to get construction leads
posts.angle: Four pools of leads. Three you own, one you rent. Rank them by cost, speed, and quality.

posts.h1_working: The free Google Business Profile (everything you get without paying anyone)
posts.angle: Contractors pay agencies $500+ for what GBP gives away. Show the full free toolkit, then say clearly what's worth paying for.

posts.h1_working: Best contractor websites of 2026 (and what makes them actually convert)
posts.angle: Pick 7 real contractor sites. Break down what works and why. Nothing is 'beautiful' — only 'converts or doesn't.'

posts.h1_working: Best marketing for contractors by trade (what works for hardscape isn't what works for HVAC)
posts.angle: Break out the big trades (hardscape, HVAC, roofing, plumbing, electrical, landscaping, painting). For each, rank what moves the needle and what's a waste.

posts.h1_working: 7 things bad contractor websites all do (and lose trust for)
posts.angle: Mirror of post 8. Seven patterns you'll see on sites that don't convert. No naming names, just screenshots of the moves.

posts.h1_working: Best SEO for contractors (what to look for in a real agency, what to run from)
posts.angle: Buyer's guide: questions to ask a real SEO agency, red flags that say run, and the three things any SEO package for a contractor must include.

posts.h1_working: Free leads for contractors: where they really come from (and what they actually cost you)
posts.angle: Nothing is free. The four sources that look free and what each one actually costs in time, quality, or margin.

posts.h1_working: AI for small business contractors: 10 real uses that actually save hours
posts.angle: Ten specific uses with the actual prompts and tools. Not 'AI will transform your business.' Each one saves a real hour.
